comment_content:
Salah scored a truck load of goals last season. Gakpo was highly effective & prolific. They are not suddenly going to fall off a cliff. It’s typical football lunacy to suggest they have lost it, or to even blame them or to axe them. The problem is compatibility & balance right now. There are too many similar players - especially in attacking positions who like to play centrally - Isak, Ekitike, Wirtz. Szobo & MacAllister are also centrally suited. Gakpo & Frimpong are the only two genuine wide players. It’s unrealistic to expect Salah at 34 years of age to start wide & create from there. He’s best suited to finish chances, & make incisive passes, which is again going to happen centrally. 
Width from fullbacks was an absolutely critical part of Klopp’s Liverpool. No team in Europe could play “football” against us, except Mancity. Everyone just played a low-block for the whole match and tried to nick a goal on the counter, even in UCL finals, or else we’d destroy them. Trent & Robbo were the ones who stretched them, creating space centrally and/or delivered expert balls into the box to rake up some of the highest number of assists in Europe, especially from fullback.
Both are suddenly gone! And this has been the biggest setback to our play. Kerkez unfortunately is raw and unready. Robbo has to come back in. I don’t know why Frimpong is not starting. Bradley’s best qualities are defensive, not offensive.

So that is Isak, Ekitike, Wirtz, Salah all requiring to play as ST/CF/CAM. Add to that Chiesa. Even at best, unfortunately 2 have to sit out. We bought Isak because he was first choice and a proven world class striker whereas nobody knew how Ekitike would perform. He got a big opportunity at the right time, so that motivated him to perform brilliantly, just like Woltemade at Newcastle. But amidst all this, we didn’t realise that we needed a Centre Back way more than anything else, because at this age Van Djik cannot cover for an error-prone partner. And if you want your fullbacks attacking up the pitch, your CBs need to be boss. Guehi would have made a HUGE difference, almost like Van Djik did in 2018. Sometimes you are just one player away from winning or losing.

Still we should have faith in Slot to rethink roles & rearrange positions for most of these players. After all it was him last season who found the correct positions for Gakpo & Gravenberch and unleashed their performances to propel us to the title. In the one season he had with them, Klopp had not figured out how to best use them.

comment_content:
United deserved all three points.  They've been solid, far more than what they've been showing in the last half a decade.  We were all over the place.

Ekitike needs to be the starting striker.  Isak is clearly not up to speed, the team doesn't play to his strenghts and as a result he just wonders around and does very little.  When Hugo is on the field, something is always happening around him.
Chiesa clearly adapted and it's time he stops being used in 10-20 minute cameos.  He brings energy, passion, revitalizes the team.  This guy is a huge talent who is finally finding his feet, give him a chance to show it for more than 20 minutes.
Robbo needs to get back to starting games, if for no other reason than to get Kerkez to up his game.  We know he can do it, he just seems scared right now.  He gets the ball and passes it back, nonstop.  Robbo plays one twos and bombs forward, Kerkez plays it safe.

The hardest part to say is obvious to everyone... Salah is off pace and might benefit starting the games on the bench until he finds that magic of his.


I had hoped this would be the game things click but they didn't.  It was clear since the start of the season that Arne will have to figure out a new system to fit all the new boys in.  When that happens, this team will be a menace.  Right now, we're up for grabs for anyone.  Might be a safe bet on Eintracht on Wednesday, even though they too are a hot mess right now.

comment_content:
গত কয়েক মৌসুম ধরে ইংলিশ প্রিমিয়ার লিগে দুকতে থাকা ফুটবল ক্লাবটি আজ লিভারপুলের গড়ের মাঠ এনফিল্ডের ৫২ হাজার অলরেড দশকদের ভিড়ে উড়তে থাকা লিভারপুলের বিপক্ষে দুর্দান্ত এক অসাধারণ জয় তুলে নিলো ইংলিশ প্রিমিয়ার লিগের আমার ফ্রেভারেইট ফুটবল ক্লাব ম্যানচেস্টার ইউনাইটেড🔥⚽💖🥰,

২০২৫-২০২৬ ইংলিশ প্রিমিয়ার লিগের এইবারের এই ফুটবল মৌসুমের আজকের ম্যানচেস্টার ইউনাইটেড বনাম লিভারপুলের মধ্যকার এই হাই-ভোল্টেজ ম্যাচের শুরুতেই মাত্র দুই মিনিটের ব্যবধানে ম্যানচেস্টার ইউনাইটেডে খেলতে থাকা ক্যামারুণের সেন্টার ফরোয়ার্ড ব্রায়ান এমবেউমোর দুর্দান্ত অসাধারণ গোলের সুবাদে লিভারপুলের এনফিল্ডের ৫২ হাজার দশকদের একেবারে স্তব্ধ ও করে দিয়ে লিভারপুলের বিপক্ষে ১-০ তে লিডে চলে যায় ম্যানচেস্টার ইউনাইটেড। আর এর পরে ম্যাচের প্রথম আর্ধ ও এই ম্যাচে দিত্বীয় আর্ধের ৭৭ মিনিট পর্যন্ত দুই দলেরেই আক্রমণ ও পাল্টা আক্রমণে পরেও এই লিড ধরে রেখেছিল ম্যানচেস্টার ইউনাইটেড,কিন্তু এই ম্যাচের ৭৮ মিনিটে আবার লিভারপুলে খেলতে থাকা ড্রাস এটাকিং মিডফিল্ডার কোডি গাপকোর গোলে এই ম্যাচে ফিরে লিভারপুল। কিন্তু এবার আর শেষ রক্ষা হলো লিভারপুলের,এই ম্যাচের শেষে দিকের ৮৪ মিনিটে ইংলিশ অভিজ্ঞ সেন্টার-ব্র্যাক হ্যারি ম্যাগুয়ারের দুর্দান্ত অসাধারণ এক হেডের গোলের ভর করে আবার ম্যাচে লিডে চলে যায় ম্যানচেস্টার ইউনাইটেড। আর তারপরে ম্যাচের বাকি সময়ের খেলায় দুই দলেরেই আক্রমণ পাল্টা আক্রমণে বাকি সময়ে আর কোন দলই গোলের দেখা না পাওয়ায় শেষ পর্যন্ত ২-১ গোলের ব্যবধানে অল-রেডদের দাপটের সাথে হারিয়ে এই এনফিল্ডে পূর্ণ ৩ পয়েন্ট তুলে নিয়ে ২০২৫-২০২৬ ইংলিশ প্রিমিয়ার লিগের পয়েন্ট টেবিলে বনমাউথ ও লিভারপুলকে টপকে এখন পর্যন্ত ৯ ম্যাচে পাঁচ  জয়ের সাথে এক ড্র আর তিন হার নিয়ে মোট ১৬ পয়েন্ট নিয়ে পয়েন্ট টেবিলের তৃতীয় স্থানে উঠে গেলো রেড-ডেবিলরা।

২০২৫-২০২৬ ইংলিশ প্রিমিয়ার লিগের এইবারের এই ফুটবল মৌসুমের আজকের এই বিগ ম্যাচে লিভারপুলের গড়ের মাঠে এনফিল্ডে লিভারপুলের বিপক্ষে ম্যানচেস্টার ইউনাইটেড ২-১ গোলের ব্যবধানে হারিয়ে দুর্দান্ত অসাধারণ এক জয় তুলে নেওয়ার জন্য আজ ইংলিশ প্রিমিয়ার লিগের আমার প্রিয় ইংলিশ ফ্রেভারেইট ফুটবল ক্লাব ম্যানচেস্টার ইউনাইটেড ফুটবল ক্লাবকে  জানাই অনেক অনেক শুভেচ্ছা ও অভিনন্দন⚽🔥💖🌹

comment_content:
We are so out of sync it is not even funny…

Firstly…Mamardashvili…decent shot stopper but he needs to start coming off his line and  punching balls clear. He just looks very shaky and that has spilled to the back line who are worried about him and can’t play with the freedom they normally do 

Secondly, Kerkez…again good player but just looks VERY out of sync…let Robbo have a run-out please! 

Thirdly, Salah…not sure what happened to him, but something about our game play has changed and it’s affected his ability to affect games. Maybe give him some time out and let Chiesa (who is playing with hunger and having an affect on proceedings) play in his position? 

Fourthly…Isak…complete COMPLETE waste of money, not because he’s a bad player, but because his profile doesn’t suit our game play the way Ekitike does and now the striker who suits us better is rotting on the bench to make way for this fellow…

Lastly…it would be easy to blame Slot but IMO the source of our issues is Richard Hughes…what was this man thinking when he gutted the spine of the team and replaced the outgoing or older players with players of completely different profiles?

comment_content:
When Jürgen Klopp was in charge and Liverpool had a full, injury-free squad, they were a team that was very hard to beat. We could always overcome strong opponents. The main problem back then was that rival teams often parked the bus, and we dropped points through draws. That’s why we developed different tactical plans to break down those defensive teams.

But the Liverpool of today loses — and not just loses, but loses badly, in a way that shows they deserved to lose. They can’t compete with their opponents. Slot needs to come up with a new plan instead of sticking with old tactics that everyone has already figured out. That’s the manager’s responsibility.

Also, I’d like to recommend a young, talented, and affordable player for scouting — Manu Koné from France. He’s a complete midfielder, better than anyone we currently have in that position.

comment_content:
Roy Keane: Van Dijk must look at himself first
 Sky Sports' Roy Keane:

"When you're a centre half and you're a big player, last year we were on about who was going to stay and he signed a big contract, then you're giving up loads of goals. I'd be looking at him and asking 'what are you doing'?

"Particularly as a big leader of the team. A couple of years ago we talked about United parking the bus here and we were critical. Now they've scored two today and two last year and you're the centre half. 

"Always start with the man in the mirror. Van Dijk, if you're the centre half and your team are all of a sudden giving up loads of goals, and you've got new people coming to your club, you've got to have a good look at yourself and think 'am I really helping people?'"

VVD stays calm after United win
Liverpool captain Virgil van Dijk after Man Utd'sñ😊😊😊 2-1 win...No mention of the first goal and h

"I think we conceded a very sloppy second goal. We worked so hard to get back into the game and we created great opportunities to score the winner but if you concede a second goal like that, that is the disappointing part.

"If you look at the game as a whole, we were far too rushed. I think they were very patient they didn't press us high but they didn't let us on the ball. We still created many opportunities to score but the reality is that we lost."

On what is next: "It is an interesting time because we have to stick together, not just us as players but as a club and the fans who want us to win."

On the first goal and whether it was a head injury: "I think the ball went long, first header was a United player, second by me. My elbow hit the player and he goes down and I went down. That angle it was difficult to score and he did. There was plenty of football left to be played [and score] and we didn't.

"We need to stay humble and stay working and keep our confidence as high as possible. When things get tough, it is important we keep the mentality of being there for each other. It is a long season."
